catalog_row.Paper: Oil supply shocks and the U.S. economy: An estimated DSGE model
catalog_row.ModelType: Medium-sized estimated U.S. DSGE; log-linear model(linear); oil and ROW blocks
catalog_row.KeyFeatures: Oil in consumption, transportation, and intermediate production; endogenous oil efficiency; domestic oil production; ROW oil supply/demand; trade and real exchange rate reduced-form blocks; Taylor rule; Rotemberg prices; real wage rigidity
catalog_row.RawPDF: raw/mmb_papers/Oil supply shocks and the U.S. economy- An estimated DSGE model.pdf

status_row.paper_title: Oil supply shocks and the U.S. economy: An estimated DSGE model
status_row.raw_pdf_path: raw/mmb_papers/Oil supply shocks and the U.S. economy- An estimated DSGE model.pdf
status_row.steady_state_quality: calibrated_share_targets_recorded; closed-form steady state not derived
status_row.notes: Primary Markdown contains model equations and calibration tables, but complete expanded log-linear system is not printed; implementation file used only as cross-check.

validation.details: English and Chinese each contain defining equation tags (F1) through (F47).

unresolved_issues: Capital-services supplier objective in the MinerU Markdown has OCR damage.
Final goods resource constraint and real GDP identity have OCR damage.
Full expanded log-linear equation list is not printed in the paper-side Markdown.
Exact AR transition equations for all U.S. shocks need source-level paper or appendix review.
No Dynare runtime validation was performed by request.
